title: What To Expect From SolarEdge's (SEDG) Q3 Earnings
text: SolarEdge Technologies Q3 2023 Earnings Preview. Revenue Expectations: Analysts were expecting SolarEdge's revenue to grow 42.1% year on year to 334.5 million, though there were slightly different forecasts from other sources. Some analysts anticipated revenue projected at 336.7 million, marking a 29.1% increase year-over-year. Profitability Expectations: Analysts anticipated an earnings per share (EPS) of -0.42, indicating the company was expected to report losses for the quarter. Historical Performance Pattern: Historically, SEDG has exceeded revenue expectations 75% of the time and EPS estimates 38% of the time over the past two years. Recent analyst activity showed 18 upward EPS revisions and 18 upward revenue revisions, indicating positive sentiment ahead of the report. Key Business Drivers: Strong revenues from the solar business, backed by increased global sales of residential batteries, power optimizers, inverters and trackers, were likely to benefit SolarEdge's sales in the third quarter, with Europe likely to have contributed significantly to Q3 revenues as installation rates continue to be high in the residential and commercial markets in the region. Earnings Release: The earnings were scheduled to be released on November 1, 2023.
